title: Fractions, Decimals, Ratios, and Proportional Structure · Mastery Check
summary: Scale recipes, medication-free training examples, mixtures, maps, and staffing plans without changing relationships. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a proportional scaling and comparison workbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent fractions as part-whole, quotient, ratio, operator, and location meanings in context.
Add, subtract, multiply, and divide fractions by reasoning about units and equivalent forms.
Convert among fractions, decimals, and terminating or repeating representations without losing meaning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Fraction Meaning evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Fraction Meaning assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Represent fractions as part-whole, quotient, ratio, operator, and location meanings in context. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.text: Name the boundaries of the idea, compare a valid case with a near miss, and trace the causal difference.

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 22 units, changes by −9 units, then by +8 units. Calculate the final quantity.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 22 − 9 + 8; compare with the starting scale.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Preserve the signs and compute 22 − 9 + 8; compare with the starting scale.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Proportion Test applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test proportionality with tables, graphs, equations, and constant ratios.

hints: Name the Proportion Test precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Fraction Meaning to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Fraction Meaning.

checks.description: The decision applies Proportion Test to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Proportion Test.

checks.description: The calculated quantity is accurate to the stated tolerance and carries its requested unit.
checks.failureMessage: Recalculate from the documented model, check the scale, and include the requested unit.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using test and proportionality plus specific case evidence.
